content.problemStatement:
For an array AAA and a number XXX, let's define f(A,X)f(A, X)f(A,X) as follows:


If it is **not possible** to split AAA into several consecutive subarrays in such a way, that the XOR of all elements in each subarray is **not equal to XXX**, f(A,X)=0f(A, X) = 0f(A,X)=0.
Otherwise, f(A,X)f(A, X)f(A,X) is equal to the **largest** possible number of subarrays in such a split.
You are given integers N,K,N, K,N,K, and X,X,X, where 0‚Č§X<2K0 \leq X \lt 2^K0‚Č§X<2K. Consider array AAA of length NNN, where each element is an integer generated uniformly from 000 to 2K‚ąí12^K - 12K‚ąí1. Find the expected value of f(A,X)f(A, X)f(A,X).

It can be shown that this value can be expressed as PQ\frac{P}{Q}QP‚Äč, where P,QP, QP,Q are non-negative integers and QQQ is not divisible by 998244353998244353998244353. Output PQ‚ąí1‚Äämod‚Ää998244353PQ^{-1} \bmod 998244353PQ‚ąí1mod998244353 (such unique value 0‚Č§R<9982443530 \le R < 9982443530‚Č§R<998244353 that RQ‚Č°P‚Äämod‚Ää998244353RQ \equiv P \bmod 998244353RQ‚Č°Pmod998244353).


Input Format

The only line of the input contains 333 integers N,K,N, K,N,K, and XXX.


Output Format
Output the expected value of f(A,X)f(A, X)f(A,X) modulo 998244353998244353998244353.

It can be shown that this value can be expressed as PQ\frac{P}{Q}QP‚Äč, where P,QP, QP,Q are non-negative integers and QQQ is not divisible by 998244353998244353998244353. Output PQ‚ąí1‚Äämod‚Ää998244353PQ^{-1} \bmod 998244353PQ‚ąí1mod998244353 (such unique value 0‚Č§R<9982443530 \le R < 9982443530‚Č§R<998244353 that RQ‚Č°P‚Äämod‚Ää998244353RQ \equiv P \bmod 998244353RQ‚Č°Pmod998244353).
